specific_concerns: The quotable line ('the senior engineer no longer reads the linter; the linter is reading her code') is witty and could be lifted to a LinkedIn post stripped of the falsifiability clause that immediately follows it. This is a packaging risk, not an analog defect — but a serious practitioner of Hindu philosophy would want the falsifiability clause to travel with the quote.

The concrete_scenario uses 'two years into the team' as a quick stand-in for the realized brahmana. This is a non-trivial flattening of the metaphysical scoping (vijānataḥ); the analog mitigates it by adding the 'announcing flood while still standing at the well' counter-figure, but a careful reader will note that 'two years' is doing a lot of compression work for what the Sanskrit names as a state, not a tenure.
verdict_reason: The engineering layer respects the verse's gravity by (a) stating the symmetric-warning structure rather than reducing the verse to 'experts skip docs', (b) providing a falsifiability clause that explicitly rejects the most common trivialization (using 'I have internalized it' as cover for not reading the linter), and (c) keeping the small-well/flood structural analogy intact rather than flattening to a slogan. The traditional meaning is preserved alongside; the engineering layer is additive. A serious practitioner would find this used-but-not-trivialized — there is no LinkedIn-tier flattening of mokṣa to productivity. Pass.
